report_name: Poland Sounds Alarm as Russian Hackers Target Water Supply Systems for Physical Disruption

threat_actors.name: State-backed actors of the Russian Federation
aliases: special services of the Russian Federation
threat_actors.description: A nation-state group attributed to the Russian Federation by Poland's ABW, conducting a campaign against Polish critical infrastructure. The group's objective shifted from espionage to physical sabotage, specifically targeting municipal water treatment facilities to disrupt public water supplies.
threat_actors.goals: To cause physical disruption and sabotage of the public water supply in Poland as part of a broader destabilization strategy.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: Attackers gained initial access to water utility networks by exploiting exposed remote services on ICS/SCADA systems connected to the public internet.

attack_patterns.name: Drive-by Compromise
attack_patterns.description: Attackers exploited weak credentials on internet-facing web panels for ICS devices to gain access.

attack_patterns.name: Manipulation of Control
attack_patterns.description: Attackers gained access to systems controlling water treatment parameters, enabling them to alter chemical dosages, change pressure levels, or shut down pumps.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Attackers gained initial access by exploiting weak or default credentials on internet-exposed ICS/SCADA systems.

identities.name: Jabłonna Lacka
identities.description: A Polish municipality whose water treatment facility was breached by Russian state-backed actors.

identities.name: Szczytno
identities.description: A Polish municipality whose water treatment facility was breached by Russian state-backed actors.

identities.name: Małdyty
identities.description: A Polish municipality whose water treatment facility was breached by Russian state-backed actors.

identities.name: Tolkmicko
identities.description: A Polish municipality whose water treatment facility was breached by Russian state-backed actors.

identities.name: Sierakowo
identities.description: A Polish municipality whose water treatment facility was breached by Russian state-backed actors.

indicators.name: ICS Port Scanning Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: ICS Port Scanning Activity
id: 2f8b1c9a-7d3e-4b0f-8c1d-9e6a5b4c3d2e
status: experimental
description: Detects network scanning activity targeting common ICS ports (Modbus 502, DNP3 20000) which could indicate reconnaissance against OT systems.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/government-and-policy/polish-abw-warns-cyberattacks-shifting-from-espionage-and-data-theft-toward-physical-disruption-of-critical-infrastructure/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-11
tags:
  - attack.t0886
  - attack.t1595
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate vulnerability scanning.
  - Misconfigured network equipment.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    dst_port:
      - 502
      - 20000
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network scanning activity targeting common ICS ports (Modbus 502, DNP3 20000) which could indicate reconnaissance against OT systems, as mentioned in the report on Polish water utility attacks.
